response.justification: The candidate is a strong and versatile engineer, but his career centers on web and full-stack development in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. His only professional C++ experience was a roughly seven-month stint at National Instruments ending in 2015, far short of the 8-12 years of hands-on C++ depth this role demands. He shows no exposure to industrial automation, IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle practices, MQTT, or message-broker architectures at scale in C++. His low-latency DLang work demonstrates relevant systems thinking, but it does not substitute for the core C++, secure-engineering, and industrial-domain requirements.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Sociomantic handling millions of requests per second
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, plus mentoring and workshop teaching experience
Broad polyglot background spanning embedded, desktop, web, and mobile with long open-source track record
concerns: Professional C++ experience is minimal and dated (~7 months at National Instruments, ending 2015)
No industrial automation, IEC 62443, SDL, MQTT, or OT-protocol experience anywhere on the resume
Recent roles are web/full-stack (TypeScript, React, Node.js), a poor match for networked multi-threaded C++ platform development

key_factors.factor: Insufficient C++ depth for a C++-centric senior role
No industrial cybersecurity or IEC 62443/SDL background
